content.problemStatement:
You want to partition the set S={1,2,…,N}S = \{1, 2, \ldots, N\}S={1,2,…,N} into KKK sets S1,S2,…,SKS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KS1​,S2​,…,SK​, such that ∣Si∣≥2|S_i| \ge 2∣Si​∣≥2, and the sum of elements in each SiS_iSi​ is odd.

Is it possible to do so?

**Note 1:** Partitioning the set S={1,2,…,N}S = \{1, 2, \ldots, N\}S={1,2,…,N} into KKK sets S1,S2,…,SKS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KS1​,S2​,…,SK​ means that every element of SSS should be in exactly one of the sets S1,S2,…,SKS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KS1​,S2​,…,SK​, and Si⊆SS_i \subseteq SSi​⊆S, for all 1≤i≤K1 \leq i \leq K1≤i≤K.

**Note 2:** ∣A∣|A|∣A∣ denotes the number of elements in the set AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers, NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if you can partition the set satisfying the requirements. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to partition the set {1,…,N} into K subsets each of size at least 2 and having an odd sum, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=5, K=2 the number of odds is 3 and evens 2. Parity condition fails, so NO. For N=14, K=5 all three conditions hold, so YES.

solution.approach:
Let O be the number of odd integers in {1,…,N} (O = ceil(N/2)) and E the number of even integers (E = floor(N/2)).
Each required subset must contain an odd number of odd elements, therefore each subset needs at least one odd number. Hence O ≥ K and the parity of the total number of odds must match the parity of K (sum of K odd numbers is odd iff K is odd). After giving one odd to each subset we have O' = O‑K remaining odds. To reach the minimum size 2, every subset needs one extra element, which can be either an even number or a pair of odds (adding two odds keeps the odd count odd). Thus the total number of “extra slots” we can fill is E + floor(O'/2). We need this to be at least K. The conditions become:
1. O ≥ K
2. O mod 2 = K mod 2
3. E + (O‑K)/2 ≥ K (the division is integer because condition 2 guarantees O‑K is even)
If all three hold, answer is YES, otherwise NO. All operations are O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
